title: guazuma guazuma var. tomentosa
intro: Guazuma guazuma var. tomentosa, also known as Guazuma or Guayacán, is a plant species in the family Malvaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Guazuma guazuma var. tomentosa include its potential to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory problems. Guazuma guazuma var. tomentosa is traditionally used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also sometimes used as a topical application to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
